faqs.question: How do I verify LiTong's authorized distributor status for Rorebai products?
faqs.answer: You can verify LiTong's authorized distributor status through multiple channels: (1) Rorebai official website - check the distributor list on Rorebai's official website; (2) Authorization certificate - LiTong can provide official authorization documents from Rorebai; (3) Direct verification - contact Rorebai directly to confirm LiTong's distributor status; (4) Product authenticity - all LiTong-supplied Rorebai products have genuine traceability. As an authorized distributor, LiTong provides full warranty support, technical assistance, and access to Rorebai's engineering resources.
faqs.decisionGuide: Purchase from LiTong with confidence knowing you are getting genuine Rorebai products with full support.

faqs.question: What is the process for requesting a quote?
faqs.answer: Requesting a quote from LiTong is straightforward: (1) Contact information - email sales@litong.com or call our sales hotline; (2) Product details - provide Rorebai part numbers, quantities, and any special requirements; (3) Application information - share your application type for appropriate product recommendations; (4) Timeline - indicate your project timeline and any forecasted volumes; (5) Response time - quotes typically provided within 24-48 hours for standard products; (6) Volume pricing - tiered pricing available for volume orders. For complex projects, our FAE team can provide technical consultation alongside the quote.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong sales with your requirements for competitive pricing and technical support.

faqs.question: How can I get technical support for Rorebai products?
faqs.answer: LiTong provides comprehensive technical support for Rorebai products through multiple channels: (1) Phone support - call our technical support hotline during business hours; (2) Email support - send technical questions to fae@litong.com; (3) Online resources - access datasheets, application notes, and FAQs on our website; (4) On-site support - available for major customers and complex projects; (5) Design review - our FAE team can review your schematic and PCB layout; (6) Failure analysis - support for any quality issues or returns. Response times vary by priority: critical issues within 4 hours, standard questions within 24 hours.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong FAE team for prompt technical support on Rorebai products.

faqs.question: What is the RMA process for defective products?
faqs.answer: LiTong's RMA process for Rorebai products: (1) Contact - reach out to support@litong.com with product details and failure description; (2) Troubleshooting - our FAE team will help diagnose the issue remotely; (3) RMA number - if confirmed defective, an RMA number will be issued; (4) Return - ship the defective product to LiTong with RMA number clearly marked; (5) Analysis - Rorebai engineering team analyzes the failure; (6) Replacement - replacement shipped within 3-5 business days after receipt. Standard warranty period is 2 years. Note: Physical damage and misuse are not covered under warranty.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong support for RMA assistance. We aim to minimize downtime with fast turnaround.

articles.title: Rorebai ADC Selection Guide
articles.summary: Comprehensive guide to selecting the right Rorebai ADC for your application.
content: This technical reference document provides detailed information about rorebai product specifications, characteristics, and performance parameters. Use this information to support your design and analysis activities.

Electrical characteristics are specified over the operating temperature range unless otherwise noted. Parameters are guaranteed by design, testing, or statistical analysis. Typical values represent the most likely parametric norm at 25°C.

Thermal characteristics require careful attention during system design. The junction-to-ambient thermal resistance depends on the mounting configuration, PCB copper area, and airflow conditions. Use thermal simulation tools to predict operating temperatures under actual conditions.

Reliability data is based on accelerated life testing and field failure analysis. Mean time between failures (MTBF) calculations follow industry-standard methodologies. Contact BeiLuo for detailed reliability reports and qualification data.

faeInsights.summary: ADC selection requires balancing resolution, speed, and power for optimal performance.
faeInsights.problemAnalysis: Many customers over-specify ADCs, leading to unnecessary cost and power consumption.
faeInsights.solutionApproach: Follow a systematic selection process considering actual requirements.
faeInsights.insight: The best ADC meets all requirements with adequate margins, not the highest specifications.
faeInsights.logic: Proper ADC selection ensures optimal system performance and cost.
keyTakeaways: Match ADC resolution to application needs
Evaluate dynamic performance for AC signals
recommendations: Use selection checklists for consistent evaluation
troubleshootingTips: Check clock quality with spectrum analyzer
faeInsights.content: Based on years of experience, this Rorebai ADC Selection Guide article addresses common challenges engineers face in project development.
faeInsights.insightLogic: Recommendations from analysis of successful product deployment projects across industries.

customerCases.problem: Selected ADC with insufficient SFDR for crowded spectrum
customerCases.diagnosis: Intermodulation products from strong adjacent channels

articles.title: PCB Layout Guide for High-Speed Analog Designs
articles.summary: Comprehensive PCB layout guidelines for Rorebai high-speed ADCs, DACs, and RF components to ensure optimal performance.
content: Proper PCB layout is critical for achieving the specified performance of high-speed analog components. This guide covers essential layout techniques for Rorebai ADCs, DACs, RF transceivers, and precision analog circuits.

Power Supply Decoupling: High-speed analog circuits require clean power supplies. Place decoupling capacitors as close as possible to device power pins. Use a combination of 100nF ceramic capacitors for high-frequency decoupling and 10µF capacitors for low-frequency bypassing. Keep power traces wide to minimize inductance.

Grounding Strategy: Implement a solid ground plane under all analog circuitry. Avoid splitting the ground plane under high-speed signal paths. Use star grounding for sensitive analog circuits to prevent ground loops. Connect analog and digital grounds at a single point near the ADC/DAC.

Signal Routing: Route high-speed differential signals (LVDS, JESD204) with controlled 100Ω differential impedance. Keep trace lengths matched within 2mm for all differential pairs. Maintain adequate spacing (>3x trace width) between critical analog signals and digital traces.

Clock Routing: Clock signals are particularly sensitive to noise and jitter. Route clock traces with proper termination and keep them away from switching signals. Use grounded guard traces on both sides of clock lines for critical applications. Minimize vias in clock paths.

Thermal Management: High-speed ADCs and RF components can generate significant heat. Use thermal vias under device packages to conduct heat to inner ground planes. Ensure adequate copper area for heat spreading. Consider forced air cooling for high-power applications.

Shielding and Isolation: For sensitive RF and precision analog circuits, implement proper shielding. Use ground planes on adjacent layers to provide natural shielding. Consider metal shields for extremely sensitive circuits in noisy environments.

faeInsights.summary: Good PCB layout is essential for achieving datasheet performance.
faeInsights.problemAnalysis: Many performance issues stem from poor layout, not component limitations.
faeInsights.solutionApproach: Follow systematic layout guidelines for power, ground, and signal routing.
faeInsights.insight: Layout quality can make or break high-speed analog performance.
faeInsights.logic: Parasitic inductance, capacitance, and coupling all impact analog performance.
recommendations: Plan for test points and debugging
bestPractices: Place decoupling caps close to pins
faeInsights.content: Based on extensive experience debugging customer designs.
faeInsights.insightLogic: Recommendations from analyzing numerous layout-related performance issues.

customerCases.diagnosis: Inadequate power decoupling and ground bounce
customerCases.solution: Redesigned with proper decoupling and solid ground plane

faqs.question: How many decoupling capacitors do I need?
faqs.answer: Decoupling capacitor guidelines: (1) Per power pin - at least one 100nF ceramic capacitor; (2) Per device - add one 10µF capacitor for bulk decoupling; (3) Per supply rail - one 1-10µF at regulator output; (4) Placement - within 2mm of power pin; (5) Types - X7R or X5R ceramic for 100nF, tantalum or ceramic for 10µF; (6) High-speed ADCs - may need additional 1nF for very high frequencies. More capacitors are generally better - the cost is minimal compared to performance impact.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 100nF per pin + 10µF per device minimum.

faqs.question: Should I use separate analog and digital ground planes?
faqs.answer: Ground plane strategy: (1) Single plane preferred - one continuous ground plane for entire board; (2) Split planes - only if absolutely necessary, connect at one point; (3) ADC connection - connect analog and digital grounds at ADC ground pin; (4) Return paths - ensure signal return paths are not blocked; (5) Current loops - minimize loop area for high-speed signals; (6) Isolation - use routing, not plane splits, for isolation. Single solid ground plane with careful component placement is usually best.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use single solid ground plane; avoid splits under signal paths.

articles.title: Power Supply Design for Precision Analog Circuits
articles.summary: Guidelines for designing clean power supplies for Rorebai precision ADCs, op-amps, and RF components.
content: Clean power supplies are essential for achieving the full performance of precision analog circuits. This application note covers power supply design considerations for Rorebai high-resolution ADCs, precision op-amps, and RF transceivers.

Noise Requirements: High-resolution ADCs are sensitive to power supply noise. A 16-bit ADC with 5V full-scale has an LSB size of 76µV. Power supply ripple should be well below this level - typically less than 1mVpp for precision applications. Use low-noise LDOs rather than switching regulators for analog supplies when possible.

LDO Selection: Select LDOs with low noise specifications (<10µVRMS) and good power supply rejection ratio (PSRR) at relevant frequencies. The LDO should have adequate PSRR (>60dB) at the ADC sampling frequency to prevent clock noise from coupling into the analog supply.

Isolation: Separate analog and digital power supplies to prevent digital switching noise from corrupting analog circuits. Use ferrite beads or small resistors between supplies to provide isolation while maintaining DC continuity. Consider separate regulators for analog and digital sections.

Sequencing: Some devices have specific power-up sequencing requirements. Ensure that digital I/O pins are not driven before the device power supply is stable. Follow datasheet recommendations for power supply sequencing and ramp rates.

Monitoring: Consider implementing power supply monitoring for critical applications. Monitor both voltage levels and current consumption to detect faults early. Some Rorebai devices include built-in supply monitoring features.

Thermal Considerations: Power dissipation in regulators affects system reliability. Calculate total power dissipation and ensure adequate heatsinking. Consider thermal shutdown features in regulator selection for safety-critical applications.

faeInsights.summary: Power supply quality directly impacts analog circuit performance.
faeInsights.problemAnalysis: Many precision issues are actually power supply problems in disguise.
faeInsights.solutionApproach: Design for low noise, good isolation, and adequate regulation.
faeInsights.insight: Invest in good power supply design - it pays dividends in performance.
faeInsights.logic: Power supply noise translates directly to measurement uncertainty.
keyTakeaways: Use low-noise LDOs for analog supplies
Separate analog and digital power domains
Ensure adequate PSRR at operating frequencies
recommendations: Measure power supply noise in system
faeInsights.content: Based on extensive power supply troubleshooting experience.
faeInsights.insightLogic: Recommendations from resolving power-related performance issues.

customerCases.problem: ADC readings showed periodic noise spikes
customerCases.diagnosis: Switching regulator noise coupling into analog supply
customerCases.solution: Replaced with low-noise LDO and added ferrite isolation

faqs.question: Can I use a switching regulator for analog supplies?
faqs.answer: Switching regulators for analog: (1) Generally not recommended - noise too high for precision; (2) If necessary - use post-regulation with LDO; (3) Synchronization - sync to ADC clock to avoid beat frequencies; (4) Filtering - extensive LC filtering required; (5) Placement - keep far from analog circuits; (6) Monitoring - check for interference with spectrum analyzer. Best practice: use dedicated LDOs for analog supplies.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use LDOs for analog; switching only with post-regulation.

faqs.question: What PSRR do I need for my ADC?
faqs.answer: PSRR requirements: (1) Calculate - PSRR > 20log(Vsupply/Vlsb) + margin; (2) 16-bit example - need >80dB PSRR for 5V supply; (3) Frequency - check PSRR at ADC sampling rate; (4) LDO selection - choose with >60dB PSRR at 1MHz; (5) Decoupling - improves effective PSRR at high frequencies; (6) Measurement - verify with injected noise. Higher resolution ADCs need better PSRR.
faqs.decisionGuide: Higher resolution requires better PSRR; check at sampling frequency.
